blocks.text: DTC P0222: Throttle opening sensor 2 voltage low

The DBW system opens and closes the throttle valve under electronic control. The system consists of a throttle actuator built into the throttle body, throttle valve and throttle position sensor 1/2, accelerator pedal position sensor 1/2, DBW relay, and PGM-FI ECU. The throttle opening degree sensor is a semiconductor device type, mounted on the throttle body, and detects the throttle valve opening degree. The throttle valve opening degree signal from the throttle opening degree sensor is input to the PGM-FI ECU and used for target opening degree feedback control. If the throttle opening degree sensor 2 output voltage remains below the set value for more than a set period of time, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
Ignition switch/ ENGINE START STOP switch
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: When the throttle position sensor 2 output voltage [throttle position sensor 2] remains below 0.3V for 0.2 seconds or longer.

items.text: Ground fault in code (THL2 line) between PGM-FI ECU and throttle position sensor 2
Disconnection of code (VCC line) between PGM-FI ECU and throttle position sensor 2

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
